prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model. Historical daily closing prices for MA from the past year were used to estimate the stock's daily drift (mean log return) and volatility (standard deviation of log returns). Specifically, the drift was calculated as the mean of log returns minus half the variance, and volatility as the standard deviation of log returns. 10,000 Monte Carlo simulations were then run for a 20-day period. The point estimate is the median of these simulated prices at the end of the 20 days. Confidence intervals are derived from the corresponding percentiles (25th/75th for 50% CI, 10th/90th for 80% CI, and 5th/95th for 90% CI) of the simulated price distribution.
